transcript.text: I'm an automated medical assistant. If this is an emergency, hang up and call 911. Do you consent to continue? (yes/no)
Yes.
What is your main medical concern today?
I'm having a lot of diarrhea.
When did your diarrhea start?
About a week ago.
Are you having any blood or mucus in your diarrhea?
Maybe a little mucus, but if there's blood, I haven't noticed.
Any fever, chills, or night sweats since your diarrhea started?
When I wipe, there's definitely no blood on the toilet paper as far as I can tell. So I I don't know. I I guess that's I guess there's no blood.
Have you noticed any recent travel, sick contacts, or changes in your diet or medications before the diarrhea started?
Well, I was around my father who has been diagnosed with C. Diff.
Have you tried any treatments or medications for your diarrhea so far?
But that I I don't know if I got anything from him.
Any abdominal pain, cramping, or bloating with your diarrhea?
I really didn't catch that.
Any recent weight loss, dehydration signs like dizziness or decreased urination, or severe weakness?
I didn't I didn't understand the the question. You asked. I guess I I heard bloating, but I don't have I guess I do have a little bit of bloating.
Have you noticed any dizziness, lightheadedness, or decreased urination recently?
But I didn't I had oh, yeah. Abdominal pain you mentioned too. So yeah, I have some bloating and abdominal pain, but I didn't catch anything else you said in that quest those questions.
Have you had any fever, chills, or night sweats since your diarrhea started?
No.
Have you noticed any signs of dehydration such as feeling very thirsty, dry mouth, or decreased urination?
I have been pretty dry, but I have also been drinking a lot, um, to compensate for it. I've been I've been using Gatorade. Too.
The next few questions may feel repetitive, but they are important for medication safety and treatment decisions. How old are you? Please say your age in years.
So that I think that's been helping.
How old are you? Please say your age in years.
Do you have any medication allergies? If yes, list them. If no, say no.
No.
What sex were you assigned at birth? Please say male or female.
No.
Is pregnancy possible? (yes/no/unknown)
No.
Have you ever been told you have kidney disease or significantly reduced kidney function? (yes/no/not sure)
No.
Have you ever been told you have liver disease (such as hepatitis, cirrhosis, fatty liver, or abnormal liver tests)? (yes/no/not sure)
No.

It sounds like you’ve been dealing with diarrhea for about a week, possibly related to an infection called C. difficile, especially since you were recently exposed to someone with this infection. Because this is a phone visit, I can’t examine you or run tests, but based on what you told me, medication treatment may be appropriate. A licensed physician will review your information and may prescribe antibiotics if needed. For now, keep drinking fluids to stay hydrated, and you can use acetaminophen or ibuprofen if you have any pain and can take them safely. 

If you notice your diarrhea getting worse, if you see blood, develop a fever, severe belly pain, or signs of dehydration like feeling very weak or dizzy, please seek in-person care the same day. If you need an exam or testing, urgent care or an emergency room can help. A doctor will review your case soon and decide on the best treatment. If you have any urgent symptoms before then, don’t wait to get care.
